They're everywhere I tell you!  They crawl in the walls, they scurry in the bushes.  Disgusting critters.  Say ... I don't suppose you're up for some sewer rat killing?  Sewer rats now, not any other kind of squeaker will do.

You there! Yes, you.  Listen, I've got a little problem on my hands, but a brave, bold hero like yourself should find it a snap to solve.  Bottom line -- we need some of the bulls in the area culled.  You're welcome to any meat or hides, and of course, I'll give you a nice reward.
Are you new around here?  Well, nevermind that.  You look ready for adventure, I can see the gleam of glory in your eyes!  Nothing is more valiant, more noble, more praiseworthy than mongbat slaying.
You failed to complete an objective in time!
Aaaahhhh!  They're everywhere!  Aaaaahhh!  Ahem.  Actually, friend, how do you feel about rabbits?  Well, we're being overrun by them.  We're finding fuzzy bunnies everywhere. Aaaaahhh!

A pity really.  With the balance of nature awry, we have no choice but to accept the responsibility of making it all right.  It's all a part of the circle of life, after all. So, yes, the boars are running rampant. There are far too many in the region.  Will you shoulder your obligations as a higher life form?

Mmm, I do love mutton!  It's slaughtering time again and my usual hirelings haven't turned up.  I've arranged for a butcher to come by and cut everything up but the basic sheep killing part I haven't gotten worked out yet.  Are you up for the task?

Pork is the fruit of the land!  You can barbeque it, boil it, bake it, sautee it.  There's pork kebabs, pork creole, pork gumbo, pan fried, deep fried, stir fried.  There's apple pork, peppered pork, pork soup, pork salad, pork and potatoes, pork burger, pork sandwich, pork stew, pork chops, pork loins, shredded pork.  So, let's get some piggies butchered!

Psst!  Hey ... psst!  Listen, I need some help here but it's gotta be hush hush.  I don't want THEM to know I'm onto them.  They watch me.  I've seen them, but they don't know that I know what I know.  You know?  Anyway, I need you to scare them off by killing a few of them.  That'll send a clear message that I won't suffer goats watching me!

Chirp chirp ... tweet chirp.  Tra la la.  Bloody birds and their blasted noise.  I've tried everything but they just won't stop that infernal clamor.  Return me to blessed silence and I'll make it worth your while.
Mighty creatures they are, aye.  Fierce and strong, can't blame 'em for wanting to feed themselves an' all.  Blame or no, they're eating all the fish up, so they got to go.  Lend a hand?
An arrow imbeds itself into your flesh!

I just can't bear it any longer.  Sure, it's my job to thin the deer out so they don't overeat the area and starve themselves come winter time.  Sure, I know we killed off the predators that would do this naturally so now we have to make up for it.  But they're so graceful and innocent.  I just can't do it.  Will you?
Well, okay. But if you decide you are up for it after all, c'mon back and see me.
You're not quite done yet.  Get back to work!
Thanks for helping me out.  Here's the reward I promised you.
You've completed a quest!  Don't forget to collect your reward.

I wish you would reconsider my offer.  I'll be waiting right here for someone brave enough to assist me.

Do you find the task distasteful?  Are you too weak to shoulder the duty of cleansing Sosaria?  So be it.
Well, where is the proof of your deed?  I will honor your actions when you have brought me the ears of the human scum.
Ah, well done.  You have chosen the path of duty and fulfilled your task with honor.
To win this war, our fighters need replacement weapons.  Bring me broadswords and I will reward you.

Well now that Moug-Guur is no more -- and I can't say I'm weeping for his demise -- it's time for the ratmen to experience a similar loss of leadership.  Slay Chiikkaha.  In return, I'll satisfy your greed temporarily.
Alright, if you'd rather not, then run along and do whatever worthless things you do when I'm not giving you direction.
How difficult is this?  The rats live in the tunnels.  Go into the tunnels and find the biggest, meanest rat and execute him.  Loitering around here won't get the task done.
It's about time!  Could you have taken longer?
Hmm, maybe you aren't entirely worthless.  I suspect a demoness of Szavetra's calibre will tear you apart ...  We might as well find out.  Kill the succubus, yada yada, and you'll be richly rewarded.
Hah!  I knew you couldn't handle it.

Amazing!  Simply astonishing ... you survived.  Well, I supposed I should indulge your avarice with a reward.
*looks around nervously*  Do you travel to The Heartwood?  I have an urgent letter that must be delivered there in the next 30 minutes -- to Ahie the Cloth Weaver.  Will you undertake this journey?

Whoever this "Dallid" might be, he's probably looking for his bucket.

Those gargoyles need to get knocked down a peg or two, if you ask me.  They're always flying over here and lobbing things at us.  What a nuisance.  Drop a dozen of them for me, would you?
Don't tell me you're a gargoyle sympathizer?  *spits*

You have awakened the master of this realm. You need to hurry to defeat it in time!
Your realm offering has reset. You will need to start over.

I understand.  The stench in those tunnels is unbearable.
The ratmen have holes all over the place that lead to their warrens.  Sometimes they're loitering around on the surface too.  Either way, they're not hard to find.

What a miserable place we live in.  Look around you at the changes we've wrought. The trees are sprouting leaves once more and the grass is reclaiming the blood-soaked soil.  Who would have imagined we'd find ourselves here?  Our "neighbors" are anything but friendly and those ogres are the worst of the lot.  Maybe you'd be interested in helping our community by disposing of some of our least amiable neighbors?
I quite understand your reluctance.  If you reconsider, I'll be here.
You can't miss those ogres, they're huge and just outside the gates here.
The unblessed failed.  Make sure there is room in your pack for the deed.

When you were exterminating those untidy vermin, you must have noticed the archers amongst them?  Those cowardly rats have been peppering us with arrows and then running away before we can retaliate.  Can I count on you to handle this for me?
Argh!  I knew those ratmen were up to no good!  They have stolen the ring I was making for my beloved Ciala.  The sparkle must have caught their greedy eyes.  Find the ring please!  I beg you.
Please, I beg of you, reconsider!
The stolen ring is easy enough to recognize.  It has an inscription on it declaring my love for Ciala.  Please find this token of love for me!
Ah my friend, thank you again for your timely aid!  The ring is finally complete and I'm ready to reveal to Ciala my love for her.  Would you ... could you present her with my token?
I know that this is a lot to ask.  Thank you for hearing my request.
My beloved Ciala is the most wonderful, most perfect, most delightful woman.  Please bring her the token quickly!  I can't bear the wait for her joyous response.
You must be standing on an arcane circle, pentagram or abbatoir to use this spell.

*sigh*  We have so much to do to clean this area up.  Even the fine work you did on those ogres didn't have much of an impact on the community.  It's the ogre lords that direct the actions of the other ogres, let's strike at the leaders and perhaps that will thwart the miserable curs.
Reluctance doesn't become a hero like you.  But, as you wish.
Ogre Lords are pretty easy to recognize.  They're the ones ordering the other ogres about in a lordly manner.  Striking down their leadership will throw the ogres into confusion and dismay!

Inconceiveable!  We've learned that the ogre leadership has recruited some heavy-duty guards to their cause.  I've never personally fought a cyclopian warrior, but I'm sure you could easily best a few and report back how much trouble they'll cause to our growing community?
Oh, I see.  *sigh*  Perhaps I overestimated your abilities.
Make sure you fully assess all of the cyclopian tactical abilities!

The ogre insurgency has taken a turn for the worse!  I've just been advised that the titans have concluded their discussions with the ogres and they've allied.  We have virtually no information about titans.  Engage them and appraise their mettle.
Certainly.  You've done enough to merit a breather.  When you're ready for more, report back to me.
Those titans don't skulk very well.  You should be able to track them easily ... their footsteps are easily the largest around.

Shhh!  *angry whisper*  This is a library, not some bawdy house!  If you wish to become a friend of the library you'll learn to moderate your volume.  And, of course, you'll take this application and have it notarized by Sarakki the Notary.  Until you've become an official friend of the library, I can't allow you to make donations.  It wouldn't be proper.
*glare*  Shhhh!  You need to visit the notary.  She can be found near the castle.

What do you have there?  Oh.  *look of dismay*  It seems everyone is interested in helping the library -- but no one warned me to stock up on sealing wax.  I'm afraid I'm out of the mixture we use to notarize offical documents.  There will be a delay ... unless you'd like to take matters into your own hands and retrieve more for me from Petrus?
I do apologize for being unprepared. Perhaps when you return later I'll have more wax in stock.
Petrus lives in Ilshenar, past the Compassion gate and beyond the gypsy camp.

What's this?  Sealing wax ... Sarakki ... official documents ... Oh, I see.  Can do, can do.  You will need to get me the poison sacs of course.  They are so volatile they aren't viable after an hour or so ... yes, yes.  Right, well off you go, speckled scorpions are the only critters that have the right poison.  They live in the desert near here.
I see. I see.  Well good luck to you.
Hello, hello!  Speckled, that's what they are.  All covered with spots and speckles when you look very closely.  Speckled scorpion poison sacs will do the trick.
The manor lord is unavailable. Please use the teleporter on your right.
Your foot brushes against a wire, tripping a hidden trap!
Your presence here has not gone unnoticed. Citadel protectors spring forth from the shadows!
The air suddenly stirs around you. From the darkness you feel the sting of the Serpent's Fang!
The floor crackles beneath your feet, alerting the protectors. The Flame of the Dragon is upon you!
From the dark corners spring the Claw of the Tiger.
The Serpent's Fang Sect leaps from the shadows with their blades drawn!
The sickening crunch under your feet alerts you of another trap filled with murderous fiends.
You hear a click behind the wall.
An arcane focus appears in your backpack.
Good good!  The wax is just cooling now and will be ready by the time you get it back to Sarakki.  You still want the wax, right?
Sorry, sorry.  I'll hold onto your order in case you change your mind.
Hello, hello.  No rush of course.  I'm sure Sarakki is patient and doesn't mind waiting for the wax.

There you are!  *pleased smile*  Don't you just love when a form is all filled in like that?  All of the sections are complete, everything is neat and tidy and the official seal is perfectly formed and in exactly the right spot.  It's comforting.  Here you are.  All you need to do now is return the form to Librarian Verity.  Have a nice day!
Friends of the Library Membership Token.
Oh dear!  You've changed your mind?  *looks flustered*  I'll file your notarized application then, in case you decide at a future date to become a friend of the library.
The librarian can always be found in the Library.  *admiring tone*  She's got a really strong work ethic.

Learning to weave spells and control the forces of nature requires sacrifice, discipline, focus, and an unwavering dedication to Sosaria herself.  We do not teach the unworthy.  They do not comprehend the lessons nor the dedication required.  If you would walk the path of the Arcanist, then you must do as I require without hesitation or question.  Your first task is to rid our home of rats ... 50 of them in the next hour.

Learning to weave spells and control the forces of nature requires sacrifice, discipline, focus, and an unwavering dedication to Sosaria herself.  We do not teach the unworthy.  They do not comprehend the lessons nor the dedication required.  If you would walk the path of the Arcanist, then you must do as I require without hesitation or question.  Your first task is to gather miniature mushrooms ... 20 of them from the branches of our mighty home.  I give you one hour to complete the task.

The way of the Arcanist involves cooperation with others and a strong committment to the community of your people.  We have run low on the cotton we use to pack wounds and our people have need.  Bring 10 bales of cotton to me.
We must look to the defense of our people!  Bring boards for new arrows.

With health and defense assured, we need look to the need of the community for food and drink.  We will feast on fish steaks, sweets, and wine.  You will supply the ingredients, the cooks will prepare the meal.  As a Arcanist relies upon others to build focus and lend their power to her workings, the community needs the effort of all to survive.

You have proven your desire to contribute to the community and serve the people.  Now you must demonstrate your willingness to defend Sosaria from the greatest blight that plagues her.  The human vermin that have spread as a disease, despoiling the land are the greatest blight we face.  Kill humans and return to me the proof of your actions. Bring me 30 human ears.
*nods* Not everyone has the temperment to undertake the way of the Arcanist.
You endanger your progress along the path with your unwillingness.
The people have need of these items.  You are proving yourself inadequate to the demands of a member of this community.
Do not falter now.  You have begun to show promise.
You must serve Sosaria with all your heart and strength.  Your unwillingness does not reflect favorably upon you.
You waste my time.  The task is simple. Kill 50 rats in an hour.
The mushrooms I seek can be found growing here in The Heartwood. Seek them out and gather them.  You are running out of time.
I care not where you acquire the cotton, merely that you provide it.
Where are the items you've been tasked to supply for the feast?
Why do you delay?  The human blight must be averted.
Every moment you procrastinate, these unnatural creatures damage Sosaria.

You have proven your desire to contribute to the community and serve the people.  Now you must demonstrate your willingness to defend Sosaria from the greatest blight that plagues her.  Unnatural creatures, brought to a sort of perverted life, despoil our fair world.  Destroy them -- 5 Exodus Overseers and 2 Exodus Minions.
The wall becomes transparent, and you push your way through it.
You must upgrade to Mondain's Legacy in order to use that item.
Your arrow hits its mark with velocity!
You have been hit by an arrow with velocity!
You already have an arcane focus.
Needs of the Many - The Heartwood

Welcome Seeker.  Do you wish to embrace your elven heritage, casting aside your humanity, and accepting the responsibilities of a caretaker of our beloved Sosaria.  Then seek out Darius the Wise in Moonglow.  He will place you on the path.

Human.  Do you crave the chance to denounce your humanity and prove your elven ancestry.  Do you yearn to accept the responsibilities of a caretaker of our beloved Sosaria and so redeem yourself.  Then human, seek out Darius the Wise in Moonglow.

*rumbling growl* *sniff* ... not-smell ... seek-fight ... not-smell ... fear-stench ... *rumble* ... cold-soon-time comes ... hungry ... eat-fish ... sleep-soon-time ... *deep fang-filled yawn* ... much-fish.

Hrrrrr. Hurrrr. Huuuman. *creaking branches* Suuun on baaark, roooooots diiig deeeeeep, wiiind caaaresses leeeaves … Hrrrrr. Saaap of Sooosaria feeeeeeds us. Hrrrrr. Huuuman leeearn. Caaaretaker of plaaants … teeend … prooove.
Hrrrr. Hrrrr.  Roooooots neeeeeed saaap of Sooosaria.  Hrrrrr.  Roooooots tiiingle neeeaaar Yeeew.  Seeeaaarch.  Hrrrr!

I greet thee human and divine my boon thou seek. Convey hence the object of my riddle and I shall reward thee with thy desire.

Three lives have I.
Gentle enough to soothe the skin,
Light enough to caress the sky,
Hard enough to crack rocks
What am I?
I give thee a hint then human.  The answer to my riddle must be held carefully or it cannot be contained at all.  Bring this elusive item to me in a suitable container.
*snort* ... guard-mates ... guard-herd *hoof stomp* ... defend-with-hoof-and-horn ... thirsty-drink.  *proud head-toss*
*impatient hoof stomp* ... thirsty herd ... water scent.

Ho, there human.  Why do you seek out the Huntsman?  The hunter serves the land by culling both predators and prey.  The hunter maintains the essential balance of life and does not kill for sport or glory.  If you seek my favor, human, then demonstrate you are capable of the duty.  Cull the wolves nearby.
Then begone. I have no time to waste on you, human.
The timber wolves are easily tracked, human.

*giggle*  So serious, so grim!  *tickle*  Enjoy life!  Have fun!  Laugh!  Be merry!  *giggle*  Find three of my baubles ... *giggle* I hid them! *giggles hysterically*  Hid them!  La la la!  Bring them quickly!  They are magical and will hide themselves again if you are too slow.
Magical baubles hidden, find them as you're bidden!  *giggle*
You weave your blade to execute a ~1_attack~.
You must learn that recipe from a scroll.

What's that? *alarmed gasp*  Do not speak of the Black Order so loudly, they might hear and take offense.  *whispers*  I collect the badges of their sects, if you wish to seek them out and slay them.  Bring five of each and I will reward you.

I have a task for you ... adventurer.  Will you risk all to win great renown?  The Black Order is organized into three sects, each with their own speciality.  The Dragon's Flame serves the will of the Grand Mage, the Tiger's Claw answers to the Master Thief, and the Serpent's Fang kills at the direction of the High Executioner.  Slay all three and you will strike the order a devastating blow!
We believe the Black Order has fallen under the sway of Minax, somehow.  Seek evidence that proves our theory by piercing the secrets of the Citadel.
You have discovered a bundle of stolen property.  Will you return it to its rightful owner?

The Dragon's Flame sect members have gone too far!  Express to them my displeasure by slaying ten of them.  But remember, I do not condone war on women, so I will only accept the deaths of men, human or elf.  Either race will do, I care not for the shape of their ears.  Yes, this action will properly make clear my disapproval and has a pleasing harmony.

I wish to make a statement of censure against the elite ninjas of the Black Order.  Deliver, in the strongest manner, my disdain.  But do not make war on women, even those that take arms against you.  It is not ... fitting.

The Tiger's Claw sect members have gone too far!  Express to them my displeasure by slaying ten of them.  But remember, I do not condone war on women, so I will only accept the deaths of men, human and elf.

The Serpent's Fang sect members have gone too far!  Express to them my displeasure by slaying ten of them.  But remember, I do not condone war on women, so I will only accept the deaths of men, human and elf.
You have discovered a contracted agreement of assassination.  Perhaps the individual named in the contract would pay gladly for the document?
*whisper* It's a very dangerous task.  Let me know if you change your mind.
*whisper* The Citadel entrance is disguised as a fishing village.  The magical portal into the stronghold itself is moved frequently.  You'll need to search for it.
I do not fault your decision.
Once you gain entrance into The Citadel, you will need to move cautiously to find the sect leaders.
Many fear to tangle with the wicked sorceress.  I understand and appreciate your concerns.
I don't know where inside The Citadel such evidence could be found.  Perhaps the most guarded sanctum is the place to look.
Of course, you could also choose to keep the bundle.
Examine the package for the name and location of its rightful owner.
The Black Order's fortress home is well hidden.  Legend has it that a humble fishing village disguises the magical portal.
Examine the contract for the name and location of the intended victim.

Why?  I ask you why?  They walk around after they're put in the ground.  It's just wrong in so many ways.  Put them to proper rest, I beg you.  I'll find some way to pay you for the kindness. Just kill five zombies and five skeletons.

Why?  I ask you why?  They walk around after they're put in the ground.  It's just wrong in so many ways.  Put them to proper rest, I beg you.  I'll find some way to pay you for the kindness. Just kill ten skeletal mounts.

Why?  I ask you why?  They walk around after they're put in the ground.  It's just wrong in so many ways.  Put them to proper rest, I beg you.  I'll find some way to pay you for the kindness. Just kill eight patchwork skeletons.

Why?  I ask you why?  They walk around after they're put in the ground.  It's just wrong in so many ways.  Put them to proper rest, I beg you.  I'll find some way to pay you for the kindness. Just kill twelve ghouls.
You can't trust them, you know.  Lizardmen I mean.  They have forked tongues ... and you know what that means.  Exterminate ten of them and I'll reward you.
They may not be bright, but they're incredibly destructive.  Kill off ten trolls and I'll consider it a favor done for me.

Those green-skinned freaks have run off with more of my livestock.  I want an orc scout killed for each sheep I lost and an orc for each chicken.  So that's four orc scouts and eight orcs I'll pay you to slay.
Disgusting!  The way they scuttle on those hairy legs just makes me want to gag.  I hate spiders!  Rid the world of twelve and I'll find something nice to give you in thanks.
It's a big job but you look to be just the adventurer to do it!  I'm so glad you came by ... I'm paying well for the death of five ogres and five ettins.  Hop to it, if you're so inclined.
I bet you can't kill ... say ten ... lava lizards!  I bet they're too much for you.  You may as well confess you can't ...
I bet you can't kill ... say twelve ... flesh golems! I bet they're too much for you. You may as well confess you can't ...
I bet you can't kill ... ten gore fiends! I bet they're too much for you. You may as well confess you can't ...
I bet you can't kill ... ten harpies!  I bet they're too much for you.  You may as well confess you can't ...
I bet you can't kill ... ten gibberlings!  I bet they're too much for you.  You may as well confess you can't ...
I bet you can't tangle with those nasty plants ... say eight corpsers and two swamp tentacles!  I bet they're too much for you.  You may as well confess you can't ...
I bet you can't tangle with those bloodsuckers ... say around ten vampire bats!  I bet they're too much for you.  You may as well confess you can't ...
You've got to help me out! Those wild ostards have been causing absolute havok around here.  Kill them off before they destroy my land.  There are around twelve of them.

It's a big job but you look to be just the adventurer to do it! I'm so glad you came by ... I'm paying well for the death of six giant ice serpents and six frost spiders. Hop to it, if you're so inclined.
You've got to help me out! Those dire wolves have been causing absolute havok around here. Kill them off before they destroy my land. They run around in a pack of around ten.
You've got to help me out! Those ratmen have been causing absolute havok around here. Kill them off before they destroy my land. I'll pay you if you kill off twelve of those dirty rats.

A pity really. With the balance of nature awry, we have no choice but to accept the responsibility of making it all right. It's all a part of the circle of life, after all. So, yes, the grizzly bears are running rampant. There are far too many in the region. Will you shoulder your obligations as a higher life form?

Have you ever seen what a slime can do to good gear?  Well, it's not pretty, let me tell you!  If you take on my task to destroy twelve of them, bear that in mind.  They'll corrode your equipment faster than anything.

I admire them, you know.  The solen have their place -- regimented, organized.  They're fascinating to watch with their constant strife between red and black.  I can't help but want to stir things up from time to time.  And that's where you come in.  Kill either twelve red or twelve black solen workers and let's see what happens next!

I imagine you don't know about the mongbats.  Well, you may think you do, but I know more than just about anyone.  You see they come in two varieties ... the stronger and the weaker.  Either way, they're a menace.  Exterminate ten of the weaker ones and four of the stronger and I'll pay you an honest wage.
Disgusting!  The way they scuttle on those hairy legs just makes me want to gag.  I hate spiders and anything like them!  Rid the world of twelve terathan drones and I'll reward you.
A gorilla once bit my sister.  No, really.  So, I'm on a crusade to have the filthy beasts destroyed once and for all.  Kill eight of them and I'll make it worth your while.
I bet you can't handle ten plague spawns!  I bet they're too much for you.  You may as well confess you can't ...

Lord Denthe disappeared after venturing into the cavern. No sign of him has been seen since.
Crystals often form in cluster-like formations in the dark recesses of the cavern.
Closed for emergency repairs. do not enter.

Presented as community-minded and strongly principled, Gargoyles have special traits and abilities:
- A Gargoyle's powerful wings carry them over land as fast as a galloping horse and grant them access to special Gargoyle-only areas.
- In situations of great danger, a Gargoyle's natural ferocity will take over, granting speed and power at the cost of defenses.
- A strong cultural emphasis on art and craftsmanship grants the Gargoyles an increased chance to imbue and unravel magical items.
- All Gargoyles are trained from childhood in the skill of Throwing, giving them a basic competence with missile weapons.
- Gargoyles have an intuitive understanding of Mysticism, allowing them to cast basic Mysticism spells without further training.
- The Stygian Abyss Expansion Pack is required to create a character of this race.
The Codex of Ultimate Wisdom embodies all that is to be known. One only has to open a page.
Moonstones were once used to travel between Trammel and Felucca.
Relvinian descended into madness while in the Hedge Maze.
Trammel and Felucca are named after Sosaria's moons.
Library Friends - Quotes from the pen of Sherry the Mouse
Oft have I wished that stranger would return.
A shard of a universe is a powerful thing.
Aid the nobility that resideth in human heart.
Yet sometimes one must sacrifice a pawn to save a king.
Even pawns have lives and loves at home, my lord.
But I am but a mouse, and none hear me.
We must bring the shards into harmony, so that they resonate in such a manner that matches the original universe.
Suddenly the shutters blew open, and Lord British fell to the ground, one hand shileding his eyes.
I witnessed them all from my tiny mousehole.
Library Friends - Quotes from the pen of Wyrd Beastmaster
I have not much to tell about dragons.
The fireball was massive enough to light a city I should surmise.
The sole time I approached one with an eye towards taming it, my initial attempts at calming it met with failure.
...the dragon suddenly whirled as if in a panic, ran a short distance, took off into the air, then transformed into a whirlwind.
I never did discover the exact cause of this strange behavior except to assume that it was not typical for this reptilian species.
Hence my basic advice to those who seek to tame a dragon-be sure that thou hast mastered the twin skills of taming animals, and running away very fast.
Library Friends - Quotes from the pen of Mercenary Justin
Be wary of a brazier that giveth warning when approached: thou canst use it to summon deadly creatures.
Deceit was once a temple to forgotten powers of old.
Do not investigate the iron maidens too closely, for they make suck you within them!
There is one place where a deadly trap can only be disarmed by making use of a statue that cleverly conceals a lever.
Oft one encounters the deadly exploding toadstool; the ones in Deceit are deadlier than most, as they explode continually.
Likewise, the very pools of water and slime on the floor may poisen thee.
The dungeon is mystically linked by crystal balls placed in different locations.
Deceit was once a temple to forgotten powers of old.
Wisps are the most intelligent of the nonhuman races inhabiting Britannia.
Library Friends - Quotes from the pen of Heigel of Moonglow
The wispish language seems to only contain one vowel, the letter Y.
The average word in wispish seems to consist of three phonemes and three glottal stops, plus possibly a pitch shift.
Wisps do make use of what sounds to us like pitch and emphasis shifts similar to exclamations and questions.
Some have speculated that what we are analyzing is in fact nothing more than the very air crackling near the wisp's glow, and not language, but this is of course unlikely.
Her name was Leyla, she said, and her hair was braided wild with creepers and thorns.
Her provenance, she told me over nights illuminated by campfires, was once the city of Trinsic.
Oft have I wondered of the boy named Japheth, and whether he remembers a girl who lived in sandstone walls.
'Twas a fearful run, and I came to the road to Yew with welts and scratches run rampant crost my skin, but I did not see her again.
He had sandy hair like thee, and I dreamt as a child of holding his hand and sharing flavored ice. His name was Japheth.
...her smile seemed full of teeth sharper than a human ought to have, for the tale of orcish raising had struck fear into the marrow of my bones.

Can you hear them? The never-ending howling? The incessant wailing? These banshees, they never cease! Never! They haunt my nights. Please, I beg you -- will you silence them? I would be ever so grateful.

Some say killing a boggling brings good luck. I don't place much stock in old wives' tales, but I can say a few dead bogglings would certainly be lucky for me! Help me out and I can reward you for your efforts.

Nobody told me about the Mummy's Curse. How was I supposed to know you shouldn't disturb the tombs? Oh, sure, now all I hear about is the curse of the vengeful dead. I'll tell you what -- make a few of these mummies go away and we'll keep this between you and me.

You want to hear about trouble? I got trouble. How's angry piles of granite walking around for trouble? Maybe they don't like the mining, maybe it's the farming. I don't know. All I know is someone's got to turn them back to potting soil. And it'd be worth a pretty penny to the soul that does it.

Please, hear me kind traveler. You know when a knight falls, sometimes they are cursed to roam the earth as undead mockeries of their former glory? That is too grim a fate for even any knight to suffer! Please, put them out of their misery. I will offer you what payment I can if you will end the torment of these undead wretches.

It is a piteous thing when the dead continue to walk the earth. Restless spirits are known to inhabit these parts, taking the lives of unwary travelers. It is about time a hero put the dead back in their graves. I'm sure such a hero would be justly rewarded.

Imps! Do you hear me? Imps! They're everywhere! They're in everything! Oh, don't be fooled by their size -- they vicious little devils! Half-sized evil incarnate, they are! Somebody needs to send them back to where they came from, if you know what I mean.

The Terathan are an aggressive species. Left unchecked, they will swarm across our lands. And where will that leave us? Compost in the hive, that's what! Stop them, stop them cold my friend. Kill their warriors and you'll check their movement, that is certain.

I've seen them hiding in their webs among the woods. Glassy eyes, spindly legs, poisonous fangs. Monsters, I say! Deadly horrors, these black widows. Someone must exterminate the abominations! If only I could find a worthy hero for such a task, then I could give them this considerable reward.

Why must the dead plague the living? With their foul necromancy and dark sorceries, the undead menace the countryside. I fear what will happen if no one is strong enough to face these nightmare sorcerers and thin their numbers.

Foul brutes! No one loves an orc, but some of them are worse than the rest. Their Captains and their Bombers, for instance, they're the worst of the lot. Kill a few of those, and the rest are just a rabble. Exterminate a few of them and you'll make the world a sunnier place, don't you know.

Thank goodness, a hero like you has arrived! Brigands have descended upon this area like locusts, stealing and looting where ever they go. We need someone to put these vile curs where they belong -- in their graves. Are you up to the task?

A Solen hive is a fascinating piece of ecology. It's put together like a finely crafted clock. Who knows what happens if you remove something? So let's find out. Exterminate a few of the warriors and I'll make it worth your while.

Kind traveler, hear my plea. You know of the evil orbs? The wrathful eyes? Some call them gazers? They must be a nest nearby, for they are tormenting us poor folk. We need to drive back their numbers. But we are not strong enough to face such horrors ourselves, we need a true hero.

Vermin! They get into everything! I told the boy to leave out some poisoned cheese -- and they shot him. What else can I do? Unless…these ratmen are skilled with a bow, but I'd lay a wager you're better, eh? Could you skin a few of the wretches for me?

The woods in these parts are restless. Wrathful trees, walking about. Why, they're a menace to decent folk! And I'm running low on firewood, so what do you say? Could you turn a few reapers into some useful lumber?

Were you the sort of child that enjoyed knocking over anthills? Well, perhaps you'd like to try something a little bigger? There's a Solen nest nearby and I bet if you killed a queen or two, it would be quite the sight to behold.  I'd even pay to see that -- what do you say?

It makes no sense! Cold blooded serpents cannot live in the ice! It's a biological impossibility! They are an abomination against reason! Please, I beg you -- kill them! Make them disappear for me! Do this and I will reward you.

The universe is all about balance my friend. Tip one end, you must balance the other. That's why I must ask you to kill not just one kind of elemental, but three kinds. Snuff out some Fire, douse a few Water, and crush some Earth elementals and I'll pay you for your trouble.
I hope you'll reconsider. Until then, farwell.
Until you kill 12 Wailing Banshees, there will be no peace.
Go back and kill all 20 bogglings!
Uh, I don't think you're quite done killing Mummies yet.
You got rocks in your head? I said to kill 12 earth elementals, okay?
Your task is not done. Continue putting the Skeleton and Bone Knights to rest.
The restless spirts still walk -- you must kill 15 of them.
Don't let the little devils scare you! You kill 12 imps -- then we'll talk reward.
Unless you kill at least 10 Terathan Warriors, you won't have any impact on their hive.
You've got a good start, but to stop the black-eyed fiends, you need to kill a dozen.
Surely, a brave soul like yourself can kill 10 Bone Magi and Skeletal Mages?
The only good orc is a dead orc -- and 4 dead Captains and 6 dead Bombers is even better!
The Brigands still plague us. Have you killed 20 of their number?
I don't think you've gotten their attention yet -- you need to kill at least 10 Solen Warriors.
Have you annihilated a dozen Gazers yet, kind traveler?
I don't see 10 tails from Ratman Archers on your belt -- and until I do, no reward for you.
I'm thinking of building a small shed and the lumber of 15 reapers would be just about right.
Dead Solen Queens isn't too much to ask, is it?
You wouldn't try and just pretend you murdered 10 Giant Ice Worms, would you?
Four of each, that's all I ask. Water, earth and fire.
Your quest is complete. Return for your reward.
Finally, some peace and quiet! You've more than earned this reward.
Well, I don't feel any luckier, but there's sure a lot less bogglings about. Good job, my friend.
Nice work. Now, here's something for your trouble -- and this will stay between you and me, right?
Well, you're turned them rocky horrors into gravel, didn't you? You've got my thanks -- and this reward.
You've done a noble deed, putting these undead down. I only wish I could give you a greater reward than this.
The spirits are now at peace. Please, take this as a reward for your work.
Oh, I hope those little buggers are burning where you sent 'em! Here's a little something to fill out your purse.
My friend, take this reward for your work in driving back the Terathan hive.
I haven't seen a single web since you cleared the area. You've more than earned this.
Pleace accept this modest offer for your bravery in facing those undead sorcerers.
Mmm. Smells like dead orc, out there! Nicely turned! Fatten your purse with this, then!
You've brought a measure of peace to this area. Please, accept this meager offering as reward.
It's fascinating to watch the nest in action, isn't it? Here's something for your trouble.
I no longer feel their prying gaze of evil upon me. For that, I give thanks to you -- here's is your reward.
Hmm, looks like you've built the better rat-trap. Enjoy a little reward then!
Yes, I like these woods much better without the trees wandering about. Nice job.
Did you seem them run around? Wasn't that great? Here, take this reward -- that’s the most fun I've had in days.
I think Giant Ice Worms are a myth, don't you? Shh, I don't want to talk about it -- just take the money and be gone.
You've restored the natural balance friend and earned this generous reward.
You have not learned this recipe.
Your reward has been placed in your backpack.

To Be or Not to Beetle
Better to Be Old and Mean

I must implore you, brave traveler, to do battle with the vile reptiles which haunt these parts. Those hideous abominations, the Ophidians, are a blight across the land. If you were able to put down a host of the scaly warriors, the Knights or the Avengers, I would forever be in your debt.

There's been a bumper crop of evil with the Bog Things in these parts, my friend. Though they are foul creatures, they are also most fecund. Slay one and you make the land more fertile. Even better, slay several and I will give you whatever coin I can spare.

Psst. Listen close my friend, for I'll not speak of this again. Unicorns. You know them, I know them. I need a number of them gone. Don't ask why something you beautiful needs to be destroyed. If you can manage the task, I have the means to reward you handsomely.

It is regretable that the Gargoyles insist upon warring with us. Their Enforcers attack men on sight, despite all efforts at reason. To help maintain order in this region, I have been authorized to encourage bounty hunters to reduce their numbers. Eradicate their number and I will reward you handsomely.

You think you've seen destruction and horror, my friend? You haven't seen anything until you've seen what a Giant Beetle can do to a nicely tilled garden. There's been a swarm of 'em lately. I don't have much, but if you could rid the area of a few, I'm sure I could find something to reward you with.

If I had but one wish, it would be to rid myself of these dread Efreet! Fire and ash, they are cunning and deadly! You look a brave soul --would you be interested in earning a rich reward for slaughtering a few of the smoky devils?

Is there anything more foul than a pixie? They have cruel eyes and a mind for mischief, I say. I don't care if some think they're cute -- I say kill them and let the Avatar sort them out. In fact, if you were to kill a few pixies, I'd make sure you had a few coins to rub together, if you get my meaning.

They aren't called Dread Spiders because they're fluffy and cuddly now, are they? No, there's nothing appealing about those wretches so I sure wouldn't lose any sleep if you were to exterminate a few. I'd even part with a generous amount of gold, I would.

Feel that chill in the air? It means an icy death for the unwary, for deadly Ice Elementals are about. Who knows what magic summoned them, what's important now is getting rid of them. I don't have much, but I'll give all I can if you'd only stop the cold-hearted monsters.

I should be trimming back the vegetation here, but something nasty has taken root. Vicious vines I can't go near. If there's any hope of getting things under control, some one's going to need to destroy a few of those Whipping Vines. Someone strong and fast and tough.

Ants are industrious and Lions are noble so who'd think an Ant Lion would be such a problem? The Ant Lions have been causing mindless destruction in these parts. I suppose it's just how ants are. But I need you to help eliminate the infestation. Would you be willing to help for a bit of reward?
They're unholy, I say. Golems, a walking mockery of all life, born of blackest magic. They're not truly alive, so destroying them isn't a crime, it's a service. A service I will gladly pay for.

It baffles me how a Ratman can be smart enough to learn magic. But, I don't think they're so smart you couldn't kill a few. The beady-eyed fiends have been causing trouble in these parts, so I'd be glad to reward someone who thinned their numbers.

The flap of their leathery wings, the scratch of their reptillian claws, the clank of their scales. Keep your eyes open, friend for there are Drakes in these parts. The unwary find themselves a hot meal for the cold blooded fiends. I'd give all the treasure I possess to anyone brave enough to scourge a number of those devils from our fair skies.

From the foul depths, the Kraken come to torment men. The scaly horrors make life near impossible for us all. I know it may not be much, but I'd gladly reward any who were fearless enough to face the Kraken and reduce their host!

I have a business proposition for you, my friend. Caravans would like to move freely across our fair deserts except the accursed Sand Vortexes always attack them. If you could, say "disenchant" a few Vortexes, I could compensate you for your efforts in encouraging free trade.

These demon horses mock the bond between rider and steed! It pains me to see such despicable monsters in the shape of a noble horse. Please, execute these unholy mockeries, the Nightmares or the Fire Steeds, for me -- do this, and I will gladly reward you.

You've heard of a snake in the grass? How about a serpent in a lava field? Those fiery monsters cause no end of trouble, slithering and burning. Find a way to turn a few lava serpents into charcoal and I'll make sure you leave with a heavy purse.

Those frosty brutes! It's impossible to make a decent bonfire with them about! I'm talking about those masters of ice magic, the Arctic Ogre Lords. Yes, I'd certainly add a few coins to the purse of anyone tough enough to turn a few of those brutes into ice cubes!

Devils of snow and ice! They steal the warmth from our very souls! If only someone were brave enough to face the dread Ice Fiends and reduce their numbers, then decent folk might have a chance for once!

From the Stygian depths, the Daemons rise to torment poor humankind! I've seen them, cutting a swath of destruction through our fair land. But you, you seem a hardy adventurous sort. Perhaps you have experience in slaying foul daemons?

Corpulent foul-smelling brutes! Without their leaders, your average ogre is such a menace. But as long as the Ogre Lords are around, they whip that unsightly rabble into shape. Hmm, makes you think we should target a few of these Orge Lords, doesn't it?

They watching me! Shh! They might hear! They've had their eyes on me for a while -- no, I'm not paranoid! Have you seen the way they look at me? Elder Gazers, indeed! Why do they observe me so? Please, kind traveler -- kill the fiends! Close their never-blinking eyes once and for all and I will gladly reward you!

My dear old mum, she does hate those awful Terathan. She'd like nothing better than to wreck their hives. Hmm, you know -- kill a Terathan Matriarch and the hive drops in efficiency. I wonder…perhaps someone who targeted a few Terathan Matriarchs would do some damage to the Hive. That'd sure make my old mum proud....

To live among giants is no easy task my friend. Take these Titans. Their appetites are prodigious, indeed. They consume all in their path trying to placate their enormous hunger! It leaves precious little for us to feed ourselves. I may not be wealthy, but if someone could kill a few Titans, I could afford to be generous with them.

You've probably slain your fair share of the common orc, eh? Orcs are a dime a dozen, but the Orc Brute -- that's a different story entirely! They're hideous monsters who've earned their deaths a dozen times over! How about giving the lumpen oafs the death they so richly deserve?

Does it seem uncommonly warm here? I think it does. I've seen Fire Gargoyles nearby. Those burning wretches turn to ash everything they touch. Do you know what does for property values? Do us all a favor and fight fire with fire -- kill some Fire Gargoyles for me.

Beware the White Wyrm, my friend! They are not a beast to be trifled with. I've seen them gut villages and swallow peasants whole! My hatred for the albino atrocities runs deep -- so deep, I'd give all that I own to rid the land of them.

Did you know Poison Elementals are corrupted from the very air? They are miserable wretches, cursed by foul magic. You do them a favor by destroying the noxious creatures -- they'd surely thank you, though you might not notice it for them trying to kill you. But, I could find it in my heart to reward you for killing poison elementals.

There is no greater foe than the mighty Dragon! A creature of myth and legend! A hero proves his worth by slaughtering the reptillian fiends! What? You're not swayed by my words? Well, would you be interested in a cash reward for killing dragons then?

Death all around us, like a plague of, uh, pestilence! Those undead magicians, the Liches, torment the living! And their masters, the Lich Lords are the worst of the bunch. I'll tell you, I'd give this large treasure pile to anyone who cleared these undead monsters!

Their girly bits are…exposed! There, out in the open, for any child to see! A succubus is an affront to all decency and morality! Someone needs to stand up for the children! Someone needs to teach the succubi a lesson in decency!
The Gargoyles think they're the superior race! Their Destroyers are the pinnacle of their warriors! But I know, humans are deadlier by far and I'll pay you to prove it to the red-skinned wretches!

The Lords of the Underworld have grown bold! They march across the land, terrorizing and rampaging! But without their leaders, the Balrons, the legions of the nether realms aren't nearly the threat. I could reward you handsomely if you could cut the head off of a demon army or two.

Oh, the cruel irony! A hero slays a dragon, only for it to come back as an undead monstrosity thanks to black magic. These Skeletal Wyrms are twice the fiend they ever were in life and it will take twice the hero to put them down again!

They say the Serpentine Dragons hail from far off lands. Well, that's just wrong. I mean, what, are native dragons supposed to compete with cheap foreign dragons? It's not fair! I think somebody should do something about it!

Every undead horror was once a living soul. Can you imagine, the poor wretches who become Rotting Corpses? What if some part of them knows what they've become? It's too horrible! Someone should return them to the ground.

The older a wyrm gets, the more deadly and cunning. The Ancient Wyrms are a treacherous breed, each one soaked in the blood of innocents. A hero needs to right their wrongs, eradicate their evil from fair Britannia.

Here's a riddle for you. Why hasn't anyone killed those annoying Sphinxes? With their puzzles and enigmatic ways, why we'd be better off without 'em all. In fact, I'll put my money where my mouth is -- kill Sphinxes and I'll reward you well.
Perhaps you'll change your mind and return at some point.
If you decide to return, I'll be waiting here.
Have you killed the Ophidian Knights or Avengers?
Continue to seek and kill the Bog Things.
There's still too many unicorns around, if you catch my drift.
I won't be able to pay you until you've gotten enough Gargoyle Enforcers.
Did you forget to kill Giant Beetles?
Those smoky devils, the Efreets, are still about.
There's too much cuteness in the world -- kill those pixies!
Dread Spiders? I say keep exterminating the arachnid vermin.
The chill won't lift until you eradicate a few Ice Elemenals.
How are farmers supposed to work with these Whipping Vines around?
Please, rid us of the Ant Lion infestation.
The unholy brutes, the Golems, must be smited!
I think someone should skin a few ratmen mages, eh?
A drake's almost a dragon but I still think you can take 'em.
Please, help stop the Kraken menace.
These Sand Vortexes make life impossible!
Don't let the Nightmares or Fire Steeds continue to walk this earth.
Those fiery serpents are still too many.
There's more Arctic Ogre Lords to kill, I'm sure of that.
I'm sure you can kill a few more Ice Fiends than that.
The Daemon hordes still need to be thinned.
Please, continue to battle the Ogre Lords.
What are you looking at? Go kill Elder Gazers!
Mum will be disappointed if you don't kill more Terathan Matriarchs.
I hope you haven't had your fill of killing Titans.
There's plenty more Orc Brutes who need killing.
Do not lose hope, you can face more Fire Gargoyles, I am certain.
The White Wyrms still trouble this land!
I beseech you, put more Poison Elementals out of their misery.
Prove you’re a hero -- kill some more Dragons!
Lich Lords still haunt our fair lands.
Do the decent thing and kill those horrible succubi!
There are plenty more Gargoyle Destroyers to, uh, destroy.
Balrons cannot be allowed to walk on the earth. Keep fighting them, please!
Reduce a few more Skeletal Wyrms to a pile of bones.
You've made a good start but those Serpentine Dragons need to be taught a lesson.
I can still smell the foul decay of Rotting Corpses.
Please, do no rest until those Sphinxes are properly schooled!
You have finished your quest and may return for a reward.
Your quest is complete. Return for your reward.
We can all rest a little easier with less of those slithery Ophidians about. You've earned this reward.
The land appreciates your efforts with the Bog Things. We should have a bumper crop this year!
Okay, here's your reward. Now, we never spoke, did we?
Those Gargoyle fanatics won't be troubling anyone any time soon, eh?
Tell me, do the Giant Beetles crunch when you kill them? Well, here's your pay.
Ha! Blown away like smoke in the wind, those Efreets are! You earned this reward!
The world is a less magical place, with dead pixies. Good job.
Thinking about those eight legged monsters gives me the chills! Here, take this reward.
You snapped that cold spell, eh? For facing the Ice Elementals, here's a little something.
With the whipping vines thinned, farmers can get back to work.
The Ant Lion infestation seems reduced. Enjoy this reward then.
Reduced those Golems to component parts? Good, then -- you deserve this reward!
Ha, the furry wretches' magic was no match for you! A dead ratman mage is worth something, so enjoy!
The skies have been clear of drakes lately, so I guess you deserve a reward.
There's hasn't been a Kraken attack lately -- congratulations!
Trade is up fifteen percent since you attacked the Sand Vortexes. You've earned a share of the profit.
You've cleared the stables of evil, my friend! So here are your just rewards!
For your work against the lava snakes, I have a little something for you.
It'll be a cold day in you-know-where when you can't kill Artic Ogre Lords, eh?
I already feel the warmth returning. Accept this reward for killing Ice Fiends!
Sent them back where they belong, eh? Good work.
The world will not miss those blubbery brutes. For what you've done, enjoy this reward.
Don't look at me that way. Just take your reward and leave.
Mother will appreciate your fine work. Here, don't tell her I gave you this.
The shadow of Titans no longer stretches across the land. Thanks to you!
Thanks to you for stopping those orcish louts. You deserve this rich reward.
You have extinguished the gargoyle threat, traveler. Thanks to you.
Those pale monsters are no more. Enjoy the rewards of the just!
You have performed a noble service. Here, please take this reward.
Dragons mean treasure my friend -- enjoy this reward!
I can only hope these undead stay in their graves once and for all.
Thanks goodness someone thinks of the children. Enjoy this token of our esteem.
The gargoyle menace has been thwarted. Thank you, traveler.
You faced the hordes of the underworld and overcame. For that, I will be forever grateful.
With any luck, those dragons won't be back again!
You sent those foreign dragons packing, didn't you? My thanks!
The stench of decay subsides, thanks to you.
You've rid the land of an ancient evil. My best wishes, friend.
Mystery solved! Enjoy your reward, my friend.

Blessings of Sosaria to you and merry met, friend.

I am glad for your company and wonder if you seek the heritage of your people?  I sense within you an elven bloodline -- the purity of which was lost when our brothers and sisters were exiled here in the Rupture.
If it is your desire to reclaim your place amongst the people, you must demonstrate that you understand and embrace the responsibilities expected of you as an elf.
The most basic lessons of our Sosaria are taught by her humblest children.  Seek Maul, the great bear, who understands instictively the seasons.
You have begun to walk the path of reclaiming your heritage, but you have not learned all the lessons before you.
You yet must perform these services:
You have carved a path in history, sought to understand the way from our sage companions.
And now you have returned full circle to the place of your origin within the arms of Mother Sosaria. There is but one thing left to do if you truly wish to embrace your elven heritage.
To be born once more an elf, you must strip of all worldly possessions. Nothing of man or beast much touch your skin.
Then you may step forth into history.

He's too busy right now, so he ignores you.
Fine then, I'm shall find another to run my errands then.
Hurry up! I don't have all day to wait for you to bring what I desire!
These human made goods are laughable! It offends so -- I must show you what elven skill is capable of!

We elves are no strangers to archery but I would be interested in learning whether there is anything to learn from the human approach. I would gladly trade you something I have if you could teach me of the deadly crossbow bolt.
I wish to try a bow crafted in the human style. Is it possible for you to bring me such a weapon? I would be happy to return this favor.
I have heard of a curious type of bow, you call it a "crossbow". It sounds fascinating and I would very much like to examine one closely. Would you be able to obtain such an instrument for me?

These human "crossbows" are complex and clever. The "heavy crossbow" is a remarkable instrument of war. I am interested in seeing one up close, if you could arrange for one to make its way to my hands.

My friend, I am in search of a device, a instrument of remarkable human ingenuity. It is a repeating crossbow. If you were to obtain such a device, I would gladly reveal to you some of the secrets of elven craftsmanship.

Humans artistry can be a remarkable thing. For instance, I have heard of a wonderful instrument which creates the most melodious of music. A lap harp. I would be ever so grateful if I could examine one in person.

How clever humans are -- to understand the need of feet to rest from time to time!  Imagine creating a special stool just for weary toes.  I would like to examine and learn the secret of their making.  Would you bring me some foot stools to examine?

Humans have remarkable desire to secure what they "possess". I would be fascinated to see how a human would choose to contain something in a wooden chest. Do you think you could bring me such an item, in exchange for knowledge of elven ways?
The manner by which humans produce cloth is remarkable. A spinning wheel -- how clever! Could you arrange for me to experiement with such a device?

I understand men decorate their living spaces with furniture such as a cherry armoire. It sounds like a beautiful, if strange invention. I would appreciate an opportunity to examine the craftsmanship on such an object.
Such a simple ingredient, yet so versatile! Sweet dough -- what can it not do? I would love to experiment and discover new frontier in the baking arts -- if only I had a supply of this sweet dough.

I have heard tales of a potent dish. One that is both strong, yet delicate. Rich, but light. A baked quiche. Such culinary wizardry sounds delightful and I would be very appreciative to one who could provide me with such a meal.
I am told of a speciality dish, a meal of carefully prepared fish. Such a sushi platter must be sublime in its delicacy. I would be eternally grateful if you could provide me with such meal.

Humans have a splendid dish I am told, one that harvests the fruits of nature and tastes like drops of the golden sun! A peach cobbler. Such a dish must be priceless, but if you could provide it for me to taste, I would happily teach you more of the elven ways.

I desire to try a simple human meal. Something hearty, but simple. Perhaps, a cut of ribs? Yes, that sounds right. Perhaps, if you were to provide this for me, I could instruct you in the ways of elven cooking?
My friend, perhaps you are interested in elven wine? It is a wondrous elixir but I require a supply of that most precious fruit, a bunch of grapes. Please, bring some to me.

I have discovered through great effort the dread after effects of human wine. My friend, I would be forever in your debt if you provide for me some of these cure potions which removes the efforts of noxious toxins.
I understand men are not unfamiliar with the winemaking arts. Does the wine of humans quench thirst like the dew off a morning lily? Perhaps I could see for myself?

We elves are interested in trading our wines with humans but we understand human usually trade such brew in strange transparent bottles. If you could provide some of these empty glass bottles, I might engage in a bit of elven winemaking

Oh, the deviltry! Why would humans lock their precious liquors inside a wooden coffin? I understand I need a "keg tap" to access the golden brew within such a wooden abomination. Perhaps, if you could bring me such a tap, we could share a drink and I could teach you.
What wonders! A concotion that let's one see in the darkest night! How marvelous! Perhaps, you would obtain for me such night sight potions so that I might banish the darkest from my eyes.

In a perilous world, one can never be too cautious. I understand mankind has developed a potion of healing to cure terrible injuries. I am certain we elves could benefit from this knowledge, so if you bring healing potions to me, I will teach you of elven lore.

These alchemy of humans is a wondrous and destructive art! I hear that an "explosive" potion produces a force of fiery potency when used. It sounds fascinating and I would like to see with my own eyes. Are you able to bring me such explosive potions?

The power of a great forrest fire contained in a simple glass container? How remarkable and terrible! I must see for myself. Could you bring for me some of these greater conflagration potions? I will, naturally, reciprocate the favor.

Human friend, tell me, is it true there is a dark side to the human art of alchemy? It is troubling to hear such tales -- I feel I must examine the toxins humans produce. Please, bring to me a supply of the deadliest poison humans craft. For this favor, I will grant you precious elven knowledge.

Most human apparel is…interesting to elven eyes. But there is one garment -- the flower garland -- which sounds very elven indeed. Could I see how a human crafts such an object of beauty? In exchange, I could share with you the wonders of elven garments.

I have heard noble tales of a fine and proud human garment. An article of clothing fit for both man and god alike. It is called a "kilt" I believe? Could you fetch for me some of these kilts so I that I might revel in their majesty and glory?

We elves believe that beauty is expressed in all things, including the garments we wear. I wish to understand more about human aesthetics, so please kind traveler -- could you bring to me magnificent examples of human fancy shirts? For my thanks, I could teach you more about the beauty of elven vestements.

Forgive me for saying this, but so much of human clothing is strange to elven eyes. For instance, the fur sarong. I have difficultry believing what I hear, so I must gaze upon it with my own two eyes. Bring some to me and I will happily repay the favor.

It is my understanding, the females of humankind actually wear on certain occasions a studded bustier? This is not simply a fanciful tale? Remarkable! It sounds hideously uncomfortable as well as ludicrously impractical. But perhaps, I simply do not understand the nuances of human clothing. Perhaps, I need to see such a studded bustier for myself?

What a thing, this human need to tinker. It seems there is no end to what might be produced with a set of Tinker's Tools. Who knows what an elf might build with some? Could you obtain some tinker's tools and bring them to me? In exchange, I offer you elven lore and knowledge.

Elves find it remarkable the human preoccupation with the passage of time. To have built instruments to try and capture time -- it is a fascinating notion. I would like to see how a clock is put together. Maybe you could provide some clocks for my experimentation?
The wonders of human invention! Turning sand and metal into a far-seeing eye! This is something I must experience for myself. Bring me some of these spyglasses friend human

Is it true human? There are those among your kind who can construct an autonomaton that mocks the shape of a man? It is a frightening but intriguing notion! I must see these golems for myself. Please, bring me a golem and I will reward you with an elven teaching.

To give voice to the passing wind, this is an idea worthy of an elf! Friend, bring me some of the amazing fancy wind chimes so that I may listen to the song of the passing breeze. Do this, and I will share with you treasured elven secrets.

I am curious to see the results of human blacksmithing. To examine the care and quality of a simple item. Perhaps, a simple bascinet helmet? Yes, indeed -- if you could bring to me some bascinet helmets, I would demonstrate my gratitude.

The clank of human iron and steel is strange to elven ears. For instance, the metallic heater shield which human warriors carry into battle. It is odd to an elf, but nevertheless intriguing. Tell me friend, could you bring me such an example of human smithing skill?
What would you say is a typical human instrument of war? Is a broadsword a typical example? I wish to see more of such human weapons, so I would gladly trade elven knowledge for human steel.

There is supposedly a terrible and impressive blade forged by the hand of man. The no-dachi, a sword which requires great smithy skills. I would be honored to view the work of such a mastery human smith. If you can assist me, I would be greatly honored.

Mankind, I am told, knows how to take the scales of a terrible dragon and forge them into powerful armor. Such a feat of craftsmanship! I would give anything to view such a creation -- I would even teach some of the prized secrets of the elven people.

Is there a finer metal than valorite? I hear tell of its wonders, but I confess I do not believe the half of it. I must see for myself. Kind human, bring me a supply of this valorite so that I might examine it more closely.

They say men greatly covet metal colored like the fair Sosarian sun. These golden ingot with their mystical properties are of great interest to the elven people. I would be greatly indebted to a human who brough me a supply of golden ingots.

Where does one get some of this horned leather, my friend? What are its properties? How difficult is it to work? Ah, perhaps these questions would be more easily answered if you simply brought me some of this horned leather. What do you say human?

I cannot help myself human. I see these balls of yarn and I wish to bat them around as if they were prey to be hunted. Please, human, bring me more of these strange and delightful balls of joys. Bring me more yarn!
Well, where are the cotton bales?

I have heard of a strange breed of magic, one that draws its power from the forces of death. This is not the elven way, but I confess my curiosity to understand more. Could you gather for me a quantity of the grave dust used in this necromantic magic?

What do humans know of archery? Humans can barely shoot straight. Why, your efforts are absurd. In fact, I will make a wager -- if these so called human arrows I've heard about are really as effective and innovative as human braggarts would have me believe, then I'll trade you something useful.  I might even teach you something of elven craftsmanship.

Human craftsmanship! Ha! Why, take an elven bow. It will last for a lifetime, never break and always shoot an arrow straight and true. Can't say the same for a human, can you? Bring me some of these human made bows, and I will show you.

Leave it to a human to try and improve upon perfection. To take a bow and turn it into a mechanical contraption like a crossbow. I wish to see more of this sort of "invention". Fetch for me a crossbow, human.

How lazy is man! You cannot even be bothered to pull your own drawstring and hold an arrow ready? You must invent a device to do it for you? I cannot understand, but perhaps if I examine a heavy crossbow for myself, I will see their appeal. Go and bring me such a device and I will repay your meager favor.

More is always better for a human, eh? Take these repeating crossbows. What sort of mind invents such a thing? I must look at it more closely. Bring such a contraption to me and you'll receive a token for your efforts.

You think you know something of music? Laughable! Take your lap harp. Crude, indelicate instruments that make a noise not unlike the wailing of a choleric child or a dying cat. I will show you -- bring lap harps, and I will demonstrate.

Human fancy knows no bounds!  It's pathetic that they are so weak that they must create a special stool upon which to rest their feet when they recline!  Humans don't have any clue how to live.  Bring me some of these foot stools to examine and I may teach you something worthwhile.

You would lock away all your possessions, fearing thieves everywhere, wouldn't you human? It is folly to put things in your wooden chests, flimsy as they are. I will show you how inferior they are to elven craftsmanship.

Thread should woven by delicate touch, not so elaborate machine as a spinning wheel. Still, there is something intriguing about the human approach. Bring some spinning wheels, so I might see how inferior thread is created.

If an elf were to create a piece of furniture, you know it would be a thing of magnificent beauty! I will show you -- bring to me a cherry armoire, what some humans would call a "beautiful" thing. Bring this, and I will point out all the ways in which it is flawed.

I have tasted human breads and I find them pathetic and lacking. Perhaps, it is the ingredients? Bring for me some of this sweet dough so that I might see how you humans go wrong. Do this and I might show you how real food is created.

I wish that humans could cook something at least half-way palatable to an elf. But alas, it is all gruel. Though these baked quiches sound promising. I expect they're awful, but you never know. Perhaps, you could bring some for me to try?

I daresay, a human would eat dirt and straw if it were served on a plate. Raw fish and weeds of the ocean? To eat a sushi platter -- what madness! I can scarcely believe it. You must bring for me a sample of such fare, so that I may see for myself.

I am told human-made peach cobbler is a dish worthy of an elf's palate. This, I very much doubt. In fact, I will prove it. Fetch for me some of these peach cobblers and then I will show you a truly magnificent dish!
You hack the ribcage off of some beast and then eat it? And call that a meal? Absurd! I will not believe it until you bring me an example of these cuts of ribs, so I might see it for myself.

Humans take perfectly wonderful grapes and reduce them to the laundry water you call wine! I will show you real elven wine. Bring to me bunches of grapes, so you might know what true craftsmen can achieve.
I have sampled human vintage and it has made me deathy ill. I require a cure for such poison. Bring me some cure potions, so I might recover from what humans call "wine".

Do not think it ego when I say elves are superior in all ways. For example, bring a bottle of your wine so that I might gag it down. If you compare it to wonderful elven vintage and you will understand.

The only good thing I can say about human made bottles is that they are empty and may yet still be filled with elven wine. Go now, fetch a number of empty bottles so that I might save them from the fate of carrying human-made wine.

I have acquired a barrel of human brewed beer. I am loathe to drink it, but how else to prove how inferior it is? I suppose I shall need a barrel tap to drink. Go, bring me a barrel tap quickly, so I might get this over with.

Why do humans want to see in the dark? Lurking in shadows to spy on others, that sounds like a human to me! Well, elves can fight fire with fire. Bring me some of these night sight potions and we will even the score.

Humans are so clumsy and stupid, always hurting themselves. I suppose that's why they have healing potions. Hmmm, I wonder what goes into such a concotion? Go and fetch me a sample, so I might investigate.
If humans are going to have explosive potions, we elves must learn more about how they are brewed. I demand you bring some of these bottles of destruction to me right away!
The mindless destruction humans cause! They really brew potions of greater conflagration! I must see for myself -- bring me some directly, and I will reward you.
I knew humans were treacherous devils. Take the deadly poisons they brew! To be used against who, I wonder? Bring me deadly poison potions, so I might have proof of human's murderous ways.
Humans do not deserve to wear a thing such as a flower garland. Help me prevent such things from falling into the clumsy hands of humans -- bring me flower garlands!
I do not believe humans are so ridiculous as to wear something called a "kilt". Bring for me some of these kilts, if they truly exist, and I will offer you meager reward.

Humans call that a fancy shirt? I would wager the ends are frayed, the collar worn, the buttons loosely stitched. Bring me fancy shirts and I will demonstrate the many ways in which they are inferior.

To murder creatures of the forrest for such a ridiculous garment as a fur sarong! It boggles the mind. Perhaps, the skins of the poor animals can be made into a proper garment. Bring me fur sarongs, so I might bring some dignity to the dead creatures.
No self respecting elf female would ever wear a studded bustier! I will prove it -- bring me such clothing and I will show you how ridiculous they are!

Bring me some of these tinker's tools! I am certain, in the hands of an elf, they will fashion objects of ingenuity and delight that will shame all human invention! Hurry, do this quickly and I might deign to show you my skill.

Elves find it remarkable the human preoccupation with the passage of time. To have built instruments to try and capture time -- it is a fascinating notion. I would like to see how a clock is put together. Maybe you could provide some clocks for my experimentation?
Humans are so pathetically weak, they must be augmented by glass and metal! Imagine such a thing! I must see one of these spyglasses for myself, to understand the pathetic limits of human sight!

Imagine! A mechanical thing which mocks a man! A golem, what an idea. I am curious about how humans create such a monstrous thing. Perhaps, I can learn more to use it against the devious humans. Go, get me a golem so I might see it more closely.
I understand humans cruely enslave the very wind to their selfish whims! Fancy wind chimes, what a monstrous idea! You must bring me proof of this terrible depredation -- hurry, bring me wind chimes!

You want to discuss the pathetic qualities of human craftsmanship? How about their bascinet helmets -- weak and flimsy things, your head would be better protect by maple leaves or a bit of straw. I will show you -- bring me some bascinet helmets and I will show you how pathetic they are.
I doubt very much a human shield would stop a good stout elven arrow. You doubt me? I will show you -- get me some of these heater shields and I will piece them with sharp elven arrows!

Pathetic, this human craftsmanship! Take their broadswords -- overgrown butter knives, in reality. No, I cannot do them justice -- you must see for yourself. Bring me broadswords and I will demonstrate their feebleness.

Some humans think their no-dachis are impressive swords. I'd rather fight with an elven quill pen in hand! I will show you how clumsy and dull they are if you bring me a few, so that we might mock their inferior workmanship.

Forging a great suit of armor from the scales of a dragon seems an idea worthy of an elf -- so I'm sure these humans have ruined it. Bring me a complete suit of this dragon armor, so I might see all the ways in which humans have done an inferior job of creation.

Valorite is one of the few metals that seems worthy of an elf's attention. With such a metal, I could craft something that would demonstrate the pinnacle of elven superiority! Get me some valorite ingots so I might forge such an item!
Humans lust so terribly for gold. Pathetic really. They barely understand what can be done with the metal. I will demonstrate what elves can craft if you'll bring some of the golden ingots.

I require a quantity of horned leather to craft something that will inspire awe, wonder and fear among the sad humans. Hurry, fetch for me this horned leather so that I might shame human craftsmen everywhere!
Here's a task even a human could do. Bring me balls of yarn. Yes, balls of yarn. Even pathetically made human yarn will do, for in the hands of an elf, wonders can be achieved.

Humans will violate the graves of their own kind? Truly? I would have thought human indencency had some boundaries, but apparently, I am wrong. Prove this is true -- bring to me some human gathered grave dust.
Ah good, you're back.  We're eager for the feast.
As you open the iron maiden, your surroundings mysteriously change!
I will take the ears you have collected now.  Hand them here.
Access to Bedlam by invitation only

Well done!  Well done, indeed.  You are worthy to become an arcanist!
I will not aid you human, unless you renounce your humanity.
Certainly.  There is plenty of time to explore such things.  Return to me should you decide the time is now.

Ah Seeker, I had not thought to see you again in that form.  Perhaps you have difficulty finding the wise Darius?  I do not know where he wanders, but I can tell you that he is always curious, so perhaps you'll find him near a great curiousity?
Well enough done.  It's best to kill quickly with respect so there isn't suffering.
Do you have them?  Let me see.
You there! I have a job for you.
Come here, I have work for you.
Hey you! Want to help me out?
Come here, I have a task.
Weakling! You are not up to the task I have.
Hey tough guy! I have a job, but I doubt you are up too it.  It requires someone far braver then you.
*sigh* Oh no. You won’t do at all.  Look at you, so weak, so puny.  Oh no no no.
Just keep walking away!  I thought so. Coward!  I’ll bite your legs off!
Ridiculous peasant!  I laugh in your general direction!  You are not up to my challenge!
You there! Yes you. Stop looking about like a toadie and come here.
You’re not Drithen! I should have known right away, just look at you. Oh well, you’ll have to do I guess...
You there, in the stupid hat!   Come here.
Excuse me! I’m sorry to interrupt but I urgently need some assistance.
Pardon me, but if you could spare some time I’d greatly appreciate it.
Might you lend a troubled soul some assistance?
Help! I need somebody! Won’t you please, please help me!
Thank goodness you are here, there’s no time to lose.
Waste not a minute! There’s work to be done.
It’s you!   I’m saved, you are just in time.
Hello friend. I realize you are busy but if you would be willing to render me a service I can assure you that you will be judiciously renumerated.
Greetings seeker.  I have an urgent matter for you, if you are willing.
Oh great adventurer, would you please assist a weak soul in need of aid?
Excuse me please traveler, might I have a little of your time?
Good day to you friend! Allow me to offer you a fabulous opportunity!  Thrills and adventure await!
I’d like to speak to an adventurer please. If I can have just a few minutes of your time, I’d like to offer you an exciting opportunity.
Hey, could you help me out with something?
Hi.  Looking for something to do?
Don’t test my patience you sniveling worm!
You there!  Yes you, the simpleton wandering aimlessly.  Come here.
I want to make you an offer you’d be a fool to  refuse.
Hey!  I want to talk to you, now.
Hello there, can I have a moment of your time?
May I call you friend?  I have a favor to beg of you.
Greetings!  I have a small task for you good traveler.
Could I trouble you for some assistance?
Have you done it yet?  Oh, I haven’t told you, have I?
My ire has been appeased by your efforts.  You are deserving of this reward.
A noble deed you have done this day.  This reward is nothing to a hero such as yourself.
You have not disappointed me.  Enjoy your reward.
